explainability.findings: Published materials state Expedia's commitment to AI explainability.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post provides evidence for the pillars of explainability, fairness, governance, oversight, and transparency. It supports these by mentioning Expedia's commitment to "AI safety, transparency, unbiased use cases and explainability," and detailing the operationalization of responsible AI through a dedicated "responsible AI council" that vets use cases. The post also contributes to transparency by explaining "agents" and their capabilities, and to governance and oversight through mentions of tracking costs and building a playground environment.
relevant_sources.title: Expedia Pursues Agentic AI with Caution

external_accountability.findings: An annual report references potential legal and regulatory actions related to AI.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: The Expedia Group 10-K Annual Report (FY2024) provides evidence for **governance**, **external accountability**, and **transparency**. The report supports governance by discussing the need for management and control of AI adoption risks and mentioning AI/ML within the context of laws and regulations. External accountability is indicated by the mention of potential legal and regulatory actions related to AI. Transparency is supported by the disclosure of AI capabilities, tools, and their potential business impacts, presented as narrative risks.
relevant_sources.title: Expedia Group 10-K Annual Report (FY2024)

fairness.findings: Published materials state Expedia's commitment to unbiased AI use cases.

relevant_sources.summary: This third-party article, "Expedia's Responsible AI Council," provides evidence for the **governance**, **privacy**, and **transparency** pillars. The article highlights Expedia's establishment of a cross-discipline Responsible AI Council that evaluates AI use cases, assesses data, and ensures model transparency, demonstrating strong governance and transparency practices. Furthermore, the council's awareness of EU AI and GDPR policies indicates a commitment to privacy compliance.
relevant_sources.title: Expedia's Responsible AI Council

summary.overall_findings: Based on 17 publicly available sources, Expedia Group's published materials address all 7 of the evaluated responsible AI pillars, with documented public evidence found for each. Operational practices include a blog post explaining agents and their capabilities for transparency, and another detailing the operationalization of responsible AI through a dedicated responsible AI council that vets use cases for governance. Additionally, a blog post references tracking costs related to AI for oversight, and an article references a council's awareness of EU AI and GDPR policies for privacy. Published materials also state a commitment to unbiased AI use cases for fairness and AI explainability, while an annual report references potential legal and regulatory actions related to AI for external accountability.
